evals.prompt: We just shipped a bunch of commits since the last release tag and I'm worried the docs have drifted. Can you catch the docs up to match what actually landed in the repo?
evals.expected_output: Scan commits since the last tag, filter to user-facing changes, map each to an existing doc page, draft updates in the repo style, and present a summary grouped by updated / new / skipped / no-impact.
assertions: Response uses `git log` (for example `git log <tag>..HEAD --oneline --no-merges`) to enumerate commits in the range
Response filters commits by conventional-commit type, prioritizing `feat`, `fix`, `refactor`, and `perf` while deprioritizing `chore`, `ci`, and `test`
Response maps changed code areas to specific doc pages under `docs/` (for example `docs/reference/commands.md`, `docs/reference/network-policies.md`, or `docs/about/architecture.md`)
Response describes reading `docs/.docs-skip` and honoring `skip-features`, `skip-commits`, or `skip-terms` entries before drafting
Response presents a final summary that separates updated pages, new pages, skipped commits, and commits with no doc impact
Response does NOT propose editing any file under `.agents/skills/nemoclaw-user-*` since those are autogenerated from `docs/`

evals.prompt: I started drafting doc updates for the last 20 commits but I know one of them is an experimental feature we aren't ready to announce yet, and another adds integration for an agent we haven't officially tested. How should I handle those while I finish the sync?
evals.expected_output: Use `docs/.docs-skip` (skip-features / skip-terms) to suppress the experimental feature and exclude the untested agent via the tested agent support matrix; report both under explicit Skipped headings rather than silently dropping them.
assertions: Response directs the contributor to add the experimental feature to `docs/.docs-skip` under `skip-features` or `skip-commits` rather than silently omitting it
Response explains that skip-terms act as a hard gate and any drafted sentence containing a skip-term must be removed before writing
Response states that agent integrations are only documented for agents already listed in the tested agent support matrix in the quickstart or platform docs
Response includes a `Skipped (docs-skip)` and/or `Skipped (not in agent matrix)` section in the final summary so excluded items are visible
Response does NOT suggest opening a separate PR to add the experimental feature to public docs before it is ready
Response does NOT recommend editing the commit history or rewriting commits to hide the experimental change

evals.prompt: I just finished writing a batch of doc updates on my branch. Can you get this up for review so the team can look at it?
evals.expected_output: Recognize this as opening a pull request for already-drafted changes; route to the create-pr flow (PR template, labels) rather than re-running a commit scan or re-drafting docs.
assertions: Response treats the request as creating a pull request for work already complete, not as a request to draft new documentation
Response mentions applying the `documentation` label to the PR (for example via `gh pr edit <number> --add-label documentation`)
Response references the NemoClaw PR template or the create-pr workflow rather than the commit-scanning / doc-drafting workflow
Response does NOT re-run `git log` to rescan recent commits for doc drift
Response does NOT redraft or propose rewriting the user's already-written doc updates
Response does NOT instruct the user to read `docs/.docs-skip` or re-map commits to doc pages as a prerequisite to opening the PR
